channel.title: Libertas - Freedom Tech Signals
channel.description: Curated insights on freedom technology: Bitcoin, privacy tools, censorship resistance, and digital sovereignty.

item.title: OONI Releases 2025 Year in Review: Major Platform Updates and Censorship Measurement Advances
item.description: OONI launched multiplatform mobile apps, thematic censorship pages, and anonymous credential systems in 2025, significantly enhancing global internet censorship measurement capabilities.

item.title: Big Tech's Data Center 'Colonialism' Sparks Community Resistance in Latin America
item.description: Major tech companies are rapidly expanding AI data centers across Latin America, creating water shortages and energy price hikes while governments fast-track approvals without community consultation.

item.title: EFF Challenges Court Secrecy in Wi-Fi Patent Case Affecting Billions
item.description: EFF forced disclosure of sealed court filings in Eastern District of Texas patent case involving Wi-Fi 6 technology, exposing widespread over-sealing that hides critical information about standard-essential patents from public view.

item.title: India Uses Anti-Terror Law to Unmask Anonymous Journalist on X
item.description: Indian police invoked anti-terrorism legislation to force X to reveal identity data of @TeluguScribe, an anonymous news outlet critical of the Telangana state government.

item.title: EFF Sues DHS/ICE Over Admin Subpoenas Targeting Online Critics
item.description: EFF sued DHS and ICE for using administrative subpoenas to unmask online critics and protest attendees, demanding transparency about these judge-free surveillance tools that target First Amendment activity.

item.title: Palantir's $130M IRS Contract Enables Mass Financial Surveillance Including Crypto Tracking
item.description: IRS has paid Palantir $130M since 2018 for data mining platform that aggregates federal databases, tracks crypto transactions, and maps social networks for financial investigations under Trump administration direction.

item.title: Tails 7.7.2 Emergency Release Fixes Critical Privilege Escalation Vulnerability
item.description: Tails released emergency update to patch Copy Fail vulnerability in Linux kernel that could allow attackers to gain admin privileges and potentially deanonymize users.

item.title: EFF Publishes DMCA Compliance Guide for Decentralized Social Platform Operators
item.description: EFF released comprehensive guidance on copyright law compliance for Mastodon, Bluesky, and other decentralized social platform operators to protect against legal liability while maintaining user protections.

item.title: Canadian Civil Society Coalition Calls for Full Withdrawal of Surveillance Bill C-22
item.description: 25 rights organizations led by Citizen Lab demand Canada withdraw Bill C-22, which would mandate year-long metadata retention and force providers to build surveillance backdoors.

item.title: Press Freedom Groups Call for Repeal of Turkey's Disinformation Law Used Against Journalists
item.description: CPJ and 22 organizations condemned Turkey's disinformation law for prosecuting journalists, calling for imprisoned reporters' release and law's repeal due to vague definitions.

item.title: New Book Documents Financial Censorship by Payment Platforms
item.description: Former EFF director's book 'Transaction Denied' exposes systematic financial censorship by PayPal, Venmo, and other platforms blocking accounts based on speech, with documented cases and successful resistance campaigns.

item.title: Infrastructure Barriers Block AI Adoption Across Sub-Saharan Africa
item.description: Poor electricity access affecting 600M Africans and expensive internet connectivity costing 25% of monthly salary create fundamental barriers to AI adoption in Sub-Saharan Africa, widening global tech inequality.

item.title: Access Now: Privacy Laws Key to Fighting Disinformation in Latin America Elections
item.description: Access Now argues that strict data protection laws are essential to combat personalized disinformation campaigns targeting voters across upcoming Latin American elections.

item.title: Kuwait Revokes Journalist's Citizenship After 2-Month Detention, Part of 71K Mass Revocations
item.description: Kuwait revoked citizenship of U.S.-Kuwaiti journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin after detaining him for journalism, part of 71,000 citizenship revocations since Oct 2024—4.6% of Kuwait's population.

item.title: Bellingcat Guide: Using China's Xiaohongshu for Open-Source Research Under Digital Oppression
item.description: Bellingcat releases comprehensive guide for investigating Chinese social platform Xiaohongshu, revealing how researchers circumvent AI surveillance and real-time censorship to track diaspora activism and expose wrongdoing.

item.title: EFF: Section 230 Critical for Open Social Web to Challenge Big Tech
item.description: EFF argues Section 230 protections are essential for decentralized social platforms like Mastodon and Bluesky to survive and compete with Big Tech monopolies.

item.title: Hack-for-Hire Campaign Targets Egyptian Journalists with Spear-Phishing Attacks
item.description: Access Now investigation exposes Asian hack-for-hire group targeting prominent Egyptian journalists and critics Mostafa Al-A'sar and Ahmed Eltantawy through sophisticated spear-phishing attacks attempting to compromise Apple and Google accounts.

item.title: Global Civil Society Adapts to AI-Driven Authoritarianism with New Tactics
item.description: Study of 10 global case studies reveals civil society using three key tactics against AI surveillance: co-opting tech for their purposes, countering digital repression, and innovating new resistance forms.

item.title: US-Israel War Strategy Targets Iran's Police Infrastructure to 'Make Iran Ungovernable'
item.description: Bellingcat investigation reveals systematic targeting of 80+ Iranian police stations and Basij paramilitary sites, part of strategy to degrade regime's 'repressive capacity' and enable civilian uprising.

item.title: GUARD Act Would Mandate ID Checks, End Anonymous Internet Use
item.description: The GUARD Act, framed as AI safety for kids, would require age verification for all users of AI-powered tools, effectively ending anonymous internet use and creating mass surveillance infrastructure.

item.title: Brazil Considers AI Deepfakes 'Excessive Risk' as Gender Violence Rises
item.description: Brazilian research center Internetlab calls for classifying non-consensual deepfakes as 'excessive risk' after surge in AI-generated sexual content targeting women and girls in schools.

item.title: Myanmar Junta Revokes Licenses of 3 Media Outlets Under Martial Law
item.description: Myanmar's military junta revoked licenses of three news outlets serving conflict-affected Chin state under martial law, forcing journalists underground and into exile operations.

item.title: WIRED Reports on Internal Conflict Within GrapheneOS Project Leadership
item.description: WIRED published an investigation into personal conflicts between key figures in GrapheneOS development, the privacy-focused Android distribution.

item.title: Mercenary Hackers Target Egyptian Opposition Journalists with Spear-Phishing Campaign
item.description: Access Now investigation reveals Asia-linked mercenary hackers targeted prominent Egyptian opposition journalists Mustafa Al-Aasar and Ahmed Al-Tantawi with sophisticated spear-phishing attacks in 2023-2024.

item.title: Reddit CLO: Section 230 Protects Decentralized Community Moderation, Not Just Big Tech
item.description: Reddit's Chief Legal Officer argues Section 230 protects volunteer moderators and community self-governance, with weakening it benefiting Big Tech while harming smaller platforms and individual users.

item.title: Ghana's Anti-LGBTQ+ Bill Would Force Social Media Censorship, Mandate Public Surveillance
item.description: Ghana's proposed Human Sexual Rights and Family Values Bill 2025 would force social media platforms to censor LGBTQ+ content and require citizens to report suspected LGBTQ+ people to authorities, creating surveillance infrastructure that threatens digital rights.

item.title: 30 Governments Used Transnational Repression in 2025, Freedom House Reports
item.description: Freedom House documented 126 new incidents of transnational repression in 2025, with 30 governments targeting critics abroad through detention, deportation, and violence.

item.title: EFF Exposes ICE Data Access After Google Broke Privacy Promise to Client
item.description: EFF reveals case where Google handed client data to ICE despite privacy promises, highlighting corporate failure to protect users from government surveillance overreach.

item.title: EFF Defends Section 230 Immunity for App Store Payment Processing
item.description: EFF argues to 9th Circuit that app stores shouldn't lose Section 230 immunity for processing payments, warning this could force widespread content censorship across platforms.

item.title: Chinese Authorities Force Malaysia to Detain and Deport Uyghur Scholar
item.description: Malaysia detained and expelled Uyghur scholar Abdulhakim Idris at China's request, preventing him from launching a book about Chinese pressure on Muslim nations to stay silent on Uyghur persecution.

item.title: Edmonton Police Deploy AI Facial Recognition on Bodycams for 'High-Risk Offenders'
item.description: Edmonton Police Service is trialing bodycam facial recognition technology targeting 'high-risk offenders,' with experts calling it Canada's most dangerous algorithmic surveillance program to date.

item.title: Nigerian Cybercrime Law Used to Target 24+ Journalists and Activists
item.description: Nigeria's 2015 Cybercrimes Act has been weaponized against 24+ journalists and activists through overbroad 'cyberstalking' provisions that criminalize critical speech.

item.title: EFF Documents Censorship and Digital Isolation of Palestinians in UN Submission
item.description: EFF submitted evidence to UN Special Rapporteur documenting increased censorship, surveillance, and attacks on Palestinian media infrastructure since October 2023.

item.title: EFF Guide: Bridge Social Media Accounts Across Mastodon, Bluesky, and Threads
item.description: EFF published a comprehensive guide on using bridging tools like Bridgy Fed to connect accounts across federated social platforms, enabling cross-platform posting and reducing platform lock-in.

item.title: Citizen Lab Research Quantifies Digital Surveillance Chilling Effects Post-Snowden
item.description: New research by Jon Penney documents measurable self-censorship behavior following Snowden revelations, showing how surveillance awareness directly reduces access to sensitive content.

item.title: Zimbabwe Targets Student Leaders Opposing Constitutional Amendment
item.description: Zimbabwean authorities are systematically abducting, torturing, and arresting student leaders protesting a constitutional amendment that would extend presidential terms from 5 to 7 years.

item.title: States Block Public Oversight of License Plate Surveillance After Abuse Revelations
item.description: Multiple US states are passing laws to block public access to ALPR surveillance data after transparency advocates used records requests to expose tracking of protesters, racist policing, and abortion seekers.

item.title: Zambia Cancels Major Digital Rights Summit RightsCon 2026 Amid Foreign Pressure
item.description: Zambia effectively canceled RightsCon 2026, a leading human rights and technology summit, citing vague administrative concerns, with reports suggesting Chinese government displeasure over Taiwanese participants influenced the decision.

item.title: EFF Opposes Insufficient FISA 702 Reauthorization Bill Lacking Warrant Requirements
item.description: EFF condemns Speaker Johnson's Foreign Intelligence Accountability Act as inadequate reform that fails to require warrants for FBI searches of Americans' communications collected under Section 702 surveillance.

item.title: Freedom House: Dictators Increasingly Collaborate on Cross-Border Dissident Hunting
item.description: Freedom House reports growing collaboration between authoritarian regimes to track and suppress dissidents across borders, escalating the threat environment for activists worldwide.

item.title: Digital Rights Movement Evolution Post-Arab Spring: From Tech Optimism to Sovereignty Focus
item.description: EFF analysis shows Middle East digital rights movement evolved from 2011 tech optimism to sophisticated focus on digital sovereignty, with organizations shifting from platform accountability to open source alternatives.

item.title: Nordic Countries Show Strongest European Resistance to US Tech Surveillance
item.description: 85% of Nordic citizens find Europe's reliance on US tech concerning, with geopolitical tensions driving preference for European alternatives to avoid NSA surveillance and FISA data collection.

item.title: China's AI Surveillance Model Goes Global Despite Security Flaws
item.description: China's AI surveillance infrastructure serves as both blueprint and warning for global authoritarianism, with similar facial recognition and monitoring tools now deployed by democratic states despite ongoing security vulnerabilities.

item.title: Age Verification Expanding Across Platforms, OS, and Government Digital ID Systems
item.description: Age verification is rapidly expanding from individual platforms to operating systems and national digital ID frameworks, creating pervasive identity-linked access control that threatens online anonymity and privacy.

item.title: Framework for Human Rights-Based AI Governance Proposed
item.description: Feminist technologist Rebecca Ryakitimbo proposes five non-negotiable rights for human-centered AI: life/liberty, equality, free speech, access to essentials, and privacy/data sovereignty.

item.title: Citizen Lab Exposes How Ad-Tech Data Enables Government Location Surveillance
item.description: New Citizen Lab investigation reveals how Webloc surveillance system leverages ad-tech data to enable government tracking, with ICE using it to circumvent legal protections.

item.title: Australian Digital Rights Leader on Social Media Bans and Post-Pandemic Institutional Distrust
item.description: Digital Rights Watch founder Lizzie O'Shea discusses Australia's strict under-16 social media ban and how post-pandemic institutional distrust complicates balancing corporate power with democratic oversight.
